title: Eriolaena linearifolia
intro: Eriolaena linearifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly found in the Himalayan region. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Eriolaena linearifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Eriolaena linearifolia is used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are commonly used to treat fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Eriolaena linearifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the spurge family. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics, which include its unique flowers and leaf arrangement. The Euphorbiaceae family is a large and diverse group, comprising over 300 genera and 7,000 species. As a member of this family, Eriolaena linearifolia shares certain traits with other plants in the Euphorbiaceae. Further classification within the plant kingdom includes its placement within the subfamily Acalyphoideae.
